question: What topics did I perform worst in last week?
query: SELECT topic, AVG(answer::int) AS success_rate FROM mathe.assessment WHERE date >= now() - interval '7 days' GROUP BY topic ORDER BY success_rate ASC;
comment: Added ORDER BY to actually return the worst-performing topics.

question: Show the progress of the Civil Engineering class in Linear Optimization compared to the Economics class.
comment: No notion of class, course, or program (Civil Engineering, Economics) exists in the schema.

question: Who are the students at risk of failing based on last month's activities?
query: SELECT student_id, success_rate FROM (SELECT student_id, AVG(answer::int) AS success_rate FROM mathe.assessment WHERE date >= now() - interval '1 month' GROUP BY student_id) AS subquery WHERE success_rate < 0.5;
comment: Assumes answer=1 means correct and <0.5 success rate implies risk of failing.

question: What condition is necessary for a linear system to have a unique solution?
comment: Conceptual math question; not answerable via SQL.

question: I didn't understand the difference between definite and indefinite integrals.
comment: Conceptual math explanation; no data query applicable.

question: I'm stuck on eigenvalues. Is there a short video that explains this?
query: SELECT pm.* FROM mathe.platform_materials pm JOIN mathe.material_type mt ON pm.type = mt.id WHERE mt.description='video' AND pm.title ILIKE '%eigenvalue%' ORDER BY pm.clicks DESC LIMIT 1;
comment: Valid query assuming material titles mention eigenvalues.

question: What should I study right after mastering the graphic method?
query: SELECT pm.* FROM mathe.platform_materials pm JOIN mathe.platform_material_keyword pmk ON pm.id=pmk.platformmaterialid JOIN mathe.platform__keywords pk ON pmk.platformkeywordid=pk.id WHERE pk.name='simplex method';
comment: Assumes pedagogical sequence (graphical → simplex) is encoded via keywords, which may not be guaranteed.

question: What is the average time it takes students to solve optimization problems?
query: SELECT AVG(a.duration) FROM mathe.assessment a JOIN mathe.platform__topic t ON a.topic = t.id WHERE t.name='Linear Optimization';
comment: Assumes duration is filled and topic names match exactly.

question: Show my performance per topic
query: SELECT topic, AVG(answer::int) AS success_rate FROM mathe.assessment WHERE student_id = 36 GROUP BY topic;
comment: Requires a bound parameter 36.

question: Show my performance per subtopic
query: SELECT subtopic, AVG(answer::int) AS success_rate FROM mathe.assessment WHERE student_id = 36 GROUP BY subtopic;
comment: Subtopic can be NULL; may need WHERE subtopic IS NOT NULL depending on usage.

question: Find all the topics in which the majority of students failed.
query: SELECT topic FROM (SELECT topic, AVG(answer::int) AS success_rate FROM mathe.assessment GROUP BY topic) AS subquery WHERE success_rate < 0.5;
comment: Interprets “majority failed” as average success rate < 50%.

question: Find all the questions in topic xxxxx where the majority of students failed (fail rate > 0.8)
query: SELECT question_id FROM (SELECT question_id, AVG(CASE WHEN answer=0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fail_rate FROM mathe.assessment WHERE topic = :topic GROUP BY question_id) AS subquery WHERE fail_rate > 0.8;
comment: Valid assuming answer=0 means incorrect.

question: Find all the questions in topic xxxxx where the majority of students answer correctly (success rate > 0.8)
query: SELECT question_id FROM (SELECT question_id, AVG(CASE WHEN answer=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS success_rate FROM mathe.assessment WHERE topic = :topic GROUP BY question_id) AS subquery WHERE success_rate > 0.8;
comment: Valid assuming binary grading in answer.

question: Find the written material most accessed by the students in the topic xxx
query: SELECT pm.* FROM mathe.platform_materials pm JOIN mathe.material_type mt ON pm.type=mt.id JOIN mathe.material_top_sub mts ON pm.id=mts.platformmaterialid WHERE mt.description='written' AND mts.platformtopicid=:topic ORDER BY pm.clicks DESC LIMIT 1;
comment: Valid; relies on clicks as proxy for access.

question: Find the video most accessed by the students in the topic xxx
query: SELECT pm.* FROM mathe.platform_materials pm JOIN mathe.material_type mt ON pm.type=mt.id JOIN mathe.material_top_sub mts ON pm.id=mts.platformmaterialid WHERE mt.description='video' AND mts.platformtopicid=:topic ORDER BY pm.clicks DESC LIMIT 1;
comment: Same assumption as above.
